elements.sent-2411-001: The search for new particles beyond the Standard Model (SM) is a cornerstone of high-energy physics, driven by unresolved questions such as the nature of dark matter, the origin of the neutrino mass, the baryogenesis, and the dark energy.
elements.sent-2411-002: Among the various extensions to the SM, new particles coupling to \( W \)-bosons represent a particularly intriguing avenue for exploration.
elements.sent-2411-003: In physics beyond the SM (BSM), there exist various types of neutral particles \( X \) which couple to \( W \)-bosons.
elements.sent-2411-004: Examples include: (i) extended gauge models with additional gauge bosons (\( Z' \))~\cite{Langacker:2008yv , which couples to \( W^+W^- \) via mixing with SM gauge bosons $\gamma$ or $Z$; (ii) extended scalar sectors like the Two-Higgs-Doublet Model (2HDM)~\cite{Branco:2011iw and supersymmetric models~\cite{Djouadi:2005gj , where the new CP-even scalar couple to \( W^+W^- \) via the mixing with the SM Higgs boson; (iii) pseudo-scalars, such as axions~\cite{Peccei:1977hh,Peccei:1977ur,Weinberg:1977ma, Wilczek:1977pj,Kim:2008hd or axion-like particles (ALPs) \( a \), which couple to \( W^+W^- \) via a dimension-five operator \( a W^+_{\mu\nu}\widetilde{W ^{-,\mu\nu \).}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-005: Besides, new neutral particles coupling to \( W \)-bosons also emerge in theoretical frameworks such as composite Higgs models \cite{Panico:2015jxa , extra-dimensional scenarios \cite{Csaki:2000zn , and dark matter models \cite{Kahlhoefer:2017dnp .}}}
elements.sent-2411-006: Since the new neutral particle \( X \) couples to di-\( W \)-bosons, it can be produced and decay via the \( X \)-\( W \)-\( W \) vertex at the Large Hadron Collider (LHC).
elements.sent-2411-007: In this study, we consider \( X \) produced in association with a \( W \)-boson through the \( s \)-channel exchange of a \( W^{(*) \) boson, followed by its decay into a \( W^+W^- \) pair, resulting in a tri-\( W \)-boson final state.}
elements.sent-2411-008: The mass of \( X \) is assumed to be greater than 170 GeV, ensuring its di-\( W \)-boson decay products are both on-shell.
elements.sent-2411-009: We choose the heavy photophobic ALP \cite{Craig:2018kne as a benchmark model in this work.}
elements.sent-2411-010: Axion models were originally proposed to address the strong CP problem in QCD~\cite{Dine:2000cj, Kim:2008hd .}
elements.sent-2411-011: In these models, the axion arises as a pseudo-Nambu-Goldstone boson from a spontaneously broken U(1) symmetry at a high energy scale, a mechanism known as the Peccei-Quinn mechanism~\cite{Peccei:1977hh,Peccei:1977ur .}
elements.sent-2411-012: In such original models~\cite{Peccei:1977hh,Peccei:1977ur,Weinberg:1977ma, Wilczek:1977pj, Kim:1979if, Shifman:1979if, Zhitnitsky:1980tq, Dine:1981rt, Hook:2019qoh, Quevillon:2019zrd, DiLuzio:2020wdo , couplings of the axion is strictly related to its mass $m_a$.}
elements.sent-2411-013: For the extended concept of ALPs, the mass and couplings of ALPs are treated as independent parameters.
elements.sent-2411-014: This flexibility significantly broadens the ALP parameter space, making them promising candidates for astrophysical and collider-based searches~\cite{Galanti:2022ijh,Choi:2020rgn,Qiu:2024muo .}
elements.sent-2411-015: At colliders, ALPs are typically studied through their interactions with SM particles~\cite{Bauer:2017ris, Dolan:2017osp, Bauer:2018uxu, Zhang:2021sio, dEnterria:2021ljz, Agrawal:2021dbo, Tian:2022rsi, Ghebretinsaea:2022djg, Antel:2023hkf, Biswas:2023ksj, Lu:2024fxs .}

elements.sent-2411-154: In this appendix, we provide detailed information about the MVA analyses performed using the TMVA package~\cite{TMVA:2007ngy .}
elements.sent-2411-155: Fig.~\ref{fig:importance illustrates the importance values of input observables as a function of \( m_a \), evaluated during the MVA training.}
elements.sent-2411-156: The importance values for BDT input variables are quantified by counting how often the variables are used to split decision tree nodes, and by weighting each split occurrence by the separation gain-squared it has achieved and by the number of events in the node~\cite{TMVA:2007ngy, Breiman:2017lcz .}
elements.sent-2411-157: The left plot highlights observables with the highest importance values, while those with the lowest importance values are displayed in the right panel.
elements.sent-2411-158: Table~\ref{tab:correlation_400gev_bg_horizontal presents the correlations between two observables for the total background and the signal with a benchmark mass $m_a = 400$ GeV.}
elements.sent-2411-159: The listed 12 pairs have strong correlations and remain stable under variations of $m_a$.
elements.sent-2411-160: Fig.~\ref{fig:correlations displays the correlations between pairs of observables for the signal as a function of \( m_a \).}
elements.sent-2411-161: The correlations for the selected pairs exhibit significant dependence on \( m_a \).
